title: La Vallée-du-Richelieu Regional County Municipality

(French pronunciation: [[la vale dy ʁiʃ(ə)ljø]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French"), *The Valley of the Richelieu*) is a [regional county municipality](/wiki/Regional_county_municipality "Regional county municipality") in the [Montérégie](/wiki/Mont%C3%A9r%C3%A9gie "Montérégie") region in southwestern [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec"), Canada. Its seat is [McMasterville](/wiki/McMasterville,_Quebec "McMasterville, Quebec").

+ [A-10](/wiki/Quebec_Autoroute_10 "Quebec Autoroute 10")   + [A-20 (TCH)](/wiki/Quebec_Autoroute_20 "Quebec Autoroute 20")   + [A-30](/wiki/Quebec_Autoroute_30 "Quebec Autoroute 30")   + [A-35](/wiki/Quebec_Autoroute_35 "Quebec Autoroute 35") | * **Principal Highways**   + [R-112](/wiki/Quebec_Route_112 "Quebec Route 112")   + [R-116](/wiki/Quebec_Route_116 "Quebec Route 116")   + [R-133](/wiki/Quebec_Route_133 "Quebec Route 133")   + [R-137](/wiki/Quebec_Route_137 "Quebec Route 137") | * **Secondary Highways**   + [R-223](/wiki/Quebec_Route_223 "Quebec Route 223")   + [R-227](/wiki/Quebec_Route_227 "Quebec Route 227")   + [R-229](/wiki/Quebec_Route_229 "Quebec Route 229") | * **External Routes**   + None |

["*Banque de noms de lieux du Québec*: Reference number 141084"](https://toponymie.gouv.qc.ca/ct/ToposWeb/fiche.aspx?no_seq=141084). *toponymie.gouv.qc.ca* (in French). [Commission de toponymie du Québec](/wiki/Commission_de_toponymie_du_Qu%C3%A9bec "Commission de toponymie du Québec").

**La Vallée-du-Richelieu** (French pronunciation: [[la vale dy ʁiʃ(ə)ljø]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French"), *The Valley of the Richelieu*) is a [regional county municipality](/wiki/Regional_county_municipality "Regional county municipality") in the [Montérégie](/wiki/Mont%C3%A9r%C3%A9gie "Montérégie") region in southwestern [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec"), Canada. Its seat is [McMasterville](/wiki/McMasterville,_Quebec "McMasterville, Quebec").

It surrounds the [Richelieu River](/wiki/Richelieu_River "Richelieu River") as the river flows north from [Lake Champlain](/wiki/Lake_Champlain "Lake Champlain") in the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") to the [Saint Lawrence River](/wiki/Saint_Lawrence_River "Saint Lawrence River") northeast of [Montreal](/wiki/Montreal "Montreal") at [Sorel-Tracy](/wiki/Sorel-Tracy "Sorel-Tracy"), [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec"). Dramatically different from the mountainous terrain to the south, the river valley is a vast plain that has been developed with extensive farmlands.

In the 21st century, the [Richelieu River](/wiki/Richelieu_River "Richelieu River") is very popular for both U.S. and Canadian recreational boaters, providing a connection that can bring boaters all the way from the outlet of the Saint Lawrence River to [New York Harbor](/wiki/New_York_Harbor "New York Harbor"). A number of old fortifications exist dating back to the 17th century; they were built by the French in an effort to try to prevent the [Iroquois](/wiki/Iroquois "Iroquois") from using the river as a way to attack the French settlers in the area. [Fort Richelieu](/wiki/Fort_Richelieu "Fort Richelieu") is at the mouth of the Richelieu River. Fort St. Louis (now [Fort Chambly](/wiki/Fort_Chambly "Fort Chambly")) at [Chambly](/wiki/Chambly,_Quebec "Chambly, Quebec"), Fort Sainte-Thérèse, and [Fort Saint-Jean](/wiki/Fort_Saint-Jean_(Quebec) "Fort Saint-Jean (Quebec)") at [Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu](/wiki/Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu "Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu"), are on the way. Fort St. Anne [Isle La Motte, Vermont](/wiki/Isle_La_Motte,_Vermont "Isle La Motte, Vermont") in [Lake Champlain](/wiki/Lake_Champlain "Lake Champlain") is near the river's source. The region is informally known as la Vallée-des-Forts.
